changes_applied.details: Created skills/no-mocking-validation-gates/scripts/scan-for-mocks.sh (123 lines, bash). Args: --project-dir (default .), --scan-scope (default 'src lib'), --fail-on-find (default true). Scans for 7 canonical anti-patterns (jest.mock, sinon.stub/spy/mock, vi.mock, Python MagicMock/mock.patch, Go gomock/mockgen, iOS OHHTTPStubs/Cuckoo, generic .stub()/.mock()) plus test-named files (*.test.*, *.spec.*, *_test.go, test_*.py). Auto-selects ripgrep if present, falls back to grep -rnE. Emits summary with per-pattern breakdown and first 20 hits with file:line. Exit 1 on matches when --fail-on-find=true, else 0.

changes_applied.fix: Add 'Automated scan' section to SKILL.md before 'What Gets Blocked'
changes_applied.details: Inserted a 4-line section pointing at scripts/scan-for-mocks.sh with recommended invocation. Does not remove any prose rules. Positions the scan as a pre-commit / CI companion to the pre-tool-use hook.

verification.self_test_note: Repo has no src/ or lib/ directories; both scopes are absent, so the scanner correctly reports 0 matches and exits 0. This matches the 'clean' expectation for validationforge.

self_review_notes: Script is 123 lines vs the target 80-100 — slightly over due to defensive arg parsing, the labeled pattern array (for readable breakdowns), and the separate test-file find pass. No dead branches; every line serves output or control flow. Could be trimmed by collapsing the LABELS array into inline echoes but that sacrifices readability in the summary. Pre-tool-use hook remains the realtime gate; this script is the batch / CI counterpart it has lacked. The script does not modify state — read-only scan.
